tools.description: List canvas files in the vault with node/edge counts

arguments.name: folder
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Folder to search (default: vault root). Example: "projects/diagrams" (string)

arguments.name: recursive
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Search subfolders (default: true) (boolean)

tools.description: Read the structure of a canvas file (nodes and edges)

arguments.name: path
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] Path to the canvas file (with or without .canvas extension) (string)

tools.description: Modify an EXISTING canvas file. Replaces nodes and/or edges arrays. Fails if canvas does not exist - use canvasManager.write to create new canvases.

arguments.name: path
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] Path to the existing canvas file (with or without .canvas extension) (string)

arguments.name: nodes
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Full nodes array (replaces existing nodes). Omit to keep existing nodes. (Array of an object)

arguments.name: edges
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Full edges array (replaces existing edges). Omit to keep existing edges. (Array of an object)

tools.description: Create a NEW canvas file. Fails if canvas already exists - use canvasManager.update to modify existing canvases.

arguments.name: path
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] Path for the new canvas file (with or without .canvas extension) (string)

arguments.name: nodes
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Initial nodes. Each node needs: type (text/file/link/group), x, y, width, height. IDs auto-generated if missing. (Array of an object)

arguments.name: edges
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Initial edges connecting nodes (Array of an object)

tools.description: Merge multiple vault files of the same type into a single output. Supports markdown (.md), PDF (.pdf), and audio (.mp3, .wav, .ogg, .webm). Audio supports concat (sequential) and mix (layered tracks with volume/fade) modes. Note: WebM audio encoding runs at real-time speed (encoding time equals audio duration).

arguments.name: files
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Vault-relative paths of files to compose. All files must be the same type. Required for concat mode (default). (Array of a string)

arguments.name: format
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] Output format. Determines which composer handles the operation. (One of: ["markdown", "pdf", "audio"])

arguments.name: outputPath
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] Vault-relative path for the output file. Parent directories are created automatically. (string)

arguments.name: separator
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Markdown only: separator between file sections. Default: "\n---\n" (string)

arguments.name: includeHeaders
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Markdown only: prepend each file's name as a heading. Default: false (boolean)

arguments.name: headerLevel
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Markdown only: heading level for file headers. Default: 2 (One of: [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6])

arguments.name: frontmatterHandling
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Markdown only: how to handle YAML frontmatter. "first" keeps only the first file's frontmatter. "merge" shallow-merges all. "strip" removes all. Default: "first" (One of: ["first", "merge", "strip"])

arguments.name: maxFileSizeMb
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Per-file size limit in MB. Default: 50 (number)

arguments.name: maxTotalSizeMb
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Aggregate size limit for all input files in MB. Default: 200 (number)

arguments.name: audioMode
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Audio only: "concat" joins files sequentially. "mix" layers tracks with individual volume/offset/fade. Default: "concat" (One of: ["concat", "mix"])

arguments.name: tracks
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Audio mix mode only: track definitions with per-track volume, offset, and fade controls. (Array of an object)

arguments.name: outputFormat
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Audio only: output encoding format. Default: "wav" (One of: ["wav", "mp3", "webm"])

arguments.name: duration
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Audio only: total output duration in seconds. Trims if shorter than composed audio. Default: full duration. (number)

arguments.name: overwrite
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] If true, overwrite existing file at outputPath. Default: false (boolean)

tools.description: List supported composition formats, their file extensions, and platform availability.
Insert new content into a note at a specific position. Does not modify existing content — use replace for that.

arguments.name: path
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] Path to the note to modify (e.g. "folder/note.md"). Do not include a leading slash. (string)

arguments.name: content
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] The text to insert into the note. For multi-line content, use newline characters (\n). This content is added without modifying any existing text. (string)

arguments.name: startLine
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] Where to insert the content (1-indexed). Use -1 to append to the end of the note. Use 1 to prepend at the beginning. Any other number inserts before that line, pushing existing content down. (number)

tools.description: Read content from a file with line range

arguments.name: path
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] Path to the file to read (string)

arguments.name: startLine
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] Start line (1-based), REQUIRED - forces intentional positioning. Use 1 to read from beginning. (number)

arguments.name: endLine
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] End line (1-based, inclusive). If omitted, reads to end of file. (number)

tools.description: Replace or delete existing content in a note. Validates that the content at the specified lines matches before making changes. If the content has moved, returns the new line numbers.

arguments.name: path
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] Path to the note to modify (e.g. "folder/note.md"). Do not include a leading slash. (string)

arguments.name: oldContent
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] The exact text currently at lines startLine through endLine that you want to replace. This is validated before any changes are made — if the content at those lines doesn't match, the tool will search the entire note to find where your content actually is and tell you the correct line numbers. To delete content, set newContent to an empty string. (string)

arguments.name: newContent
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] The text to replace oldContent with. Set to an empty string to delete the content at the specified lines. (string)

arguments.name: startLine
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] The line number (1-indexed) where oldContent begins. Required — ensures the correct occurrence is targeted when identical content appears multiple times in a note. (number)

arguments.name: endLine
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] The line number (1-indexed) where oldContent ends (inclusive). Required — defines the exact range to validate and replace. (number)

tools.description: Set a frontmatter property on a note. Supports "replace" (default) and "merge" (array union with dedup) modes.

arguments.name: path
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] Path to the note file (string)

arguments.name: property
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] Frontmatter property name (e.g. "tags", "aliases", "status") (string)

arguments.name: value
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] Value to set. Can be a string, number, boolean, or array of strings.

arguments.name: mode
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] How to apply the value. 'replace' overwrites (default). 'merge' performs array union with dedup for list fields; equivalent to replace for scalars. (One of: ["replace", "merge"])

tools.description: Create a new file or overwrite existing file

arguments.name: path
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] Path to the file to create or overwrite (string)

arguments.name: content
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] Content to write to the file (string)

arguments.name: overwrite
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Overwrite if file exists (default: false) (boolean)

tools.description: Generate music from a text prompt describing genre, mood, instruments, tempo, and lyrics. Saves audio to vault.

arguments.name: prompt
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] Text description of the music to generate. Can define genre, mood, instruments, vocals, tempo, structure, and lyrics (max 4100 chars). Do NOT reference copyrighted artists or lyrics. (string)

arguments.name: musicLengthMs
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Length of the song in milliseconds (3000-600000, i.e. 3s to 10min). If omitted, the model chooses based on the prompt. (number)

arguments.name: outputPath
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Output file path in vault (default: audio/music-{timestamp}.mp3) (string)

arguments.name: outputFormat
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Audio format (default: mp3_44100_128). Options: mp3_22050_32, mp3_44100_64, mp3_44100_128, mp3_44100_192 (string)

arguments.name: forceInstrumental
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] If true, guarantees instrumental output with no vocals (default: false) (boolean)

tools.description: List available ElevenLabs voices with their IDs, names, and categories.

arguments.name: category
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Filter by category: premade, cloned, generated, professional (One of: ["premade", "cloned", "generated", "professional"])

tools.description: Generate cinematic sound effects from text descriptions (e.g., "thunder rolling across a mountain valley").

arguments.name: prompt
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] Text description of the sound effect to generate (e.g., "ocean waves crashing on rocks") (string)

arguments.name: durationSeconds
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Duration in seconds (0.5-30). If omitted, optimal duration is guessed from prompt. (number)

arguments.name: promptInfluence
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] How closely to follow the text prompt (0.0-1.0) (number)

arguments.name: outputPath
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Output file path in vault (default: audio/sfx-{timestamp}.mp3) (string)

tools.description: Convert text to speech audio using ElevenLabs voices. Saves MP3 to vault.

arguments.name: prompt
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] Text prompt to convert to speech. Supports dialogue tags like [fearful], [whispered], etc. (string)

arguments.name: voiceId
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] ElevenLabs voice ID (use listVoices to find IDs). Defaults to Sarah. (string)

arguments.name: modelId
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Model ID. Options: eleven_multilingual_v2 (default), eleven_turbo_v2 (faster), eleven_monolingual_v1 (string)

arguments.name: outputPath
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Output file path in vault (default: audio/tts-{timestamp}.mp3) (string)

arguments.name: stability
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Voice stability 0.0-1.0 (default: 0.5) (number)

arguments.name: similarityBoost
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Voice similarity boost 0.0-1.0 (default: 0.75) (number)

tools.description: List available OCR models and audio transcription models that the ingest pipeline supports. Use before ingest to discover valid provider/model options.

Ingest a PDF or audio file into a structured markdown note. PDF: text extraction (default) or vision OCR. Audio: speech transcription using explicit ingest-supported models. Output note created alongside the original file.

arguments.name: filePath
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] Path to the file to ingest (PDF, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, or audio). Relative to vault root. (string)

arguments.name: mode
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] PDF processing mode only. "text" = free text extraction (default). "vision" = OCR via LLM vision (requires ocrProvider + ocrModel). (One of: ["text", "vision"])

arguments.name: ocrProvider
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Provider for vision-based OCR (e.g., "openai", "anthropic", "google", "ollama"). Required when mode="vision". (string)

arguments.name: ocrModel
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Model for vision-based OCR (e.g., "gpt-4o", "claude-sonnet-4-6"). Required when mode="vision". (string)

tools.description: Archive a workspace (soft delete). Workspace will be hidden from lists but can be restored.

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] Name of the workspace to archive or restore (REQUIRED) (string)

arguments.name: restore
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] If true, restores the workspace from archive. If false/omitted, archives the workspace. (boolean)

tools.description: Create a state with restoration context for later resumption

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] Short descriptive title for this save point (string)

arguments.name: conversationContext
arguments.description:
[REQUIRED] Detailed markdown summary enabling seamless resumption. Structure with headings:

## Original Request
What the user asked for and their goal

## Key Decisions Made
- Decision and reasoning
- Decision and reasoning

## Important Context
Background info, constraints, or discoveries from the conversation

## Current Status
What is complete, what is in progress, any blockers (string)

arguments.name: activeTask
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] The specific task in progress when saving (string)

arguments.name: activeFiles
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] File paths being edited or referenced (Array of a string)

arguments.name: nextSteps
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] Specific action items to resume work, in priority order (Array of a string)

arguments.name: tags
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Optional tags for categorization (Array of a string)

tools.description: Create a new workspace with structured context data

arguments.name: name

arguments.name: description

arguments.name: rootFolder

arguments.name: purpose

arguments.name: workflows

arguments.name: keyFiles

arguments.name: preferences

arguments.name: dedicatedAgentId

arguments.name: relatedFolders

arguments.name: relatedFiles

arguments.name: keyFileInstructions
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Instructions for working with key files (string)

tools.description: List states with optional filtering and sorting

arguments.name: includeArchived
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Include archived states (default: false) (boolean)

arguments.name: tags

arguments.name: page

arguments.name: order

tools.description: List available workspaces with filters and sorting

arguments.name: includeArchived

tools.description: Load a saved workspace-scoped state with restored context

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] Name of the state to load (REQUIRED). Use listStates to see available states. (string)

tools.description: Load a workspace by ID and restore context and state

arguments.name: id
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] Workspace ID or name to load (REQUIRED). Accepts either the unique workspace ID or the workspace name. Using the name returned by create-workspace is fine; you do not need to call list-workspaces just to find the UUID. (string)

arguments.name: limit
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Optional limit for sessions, states, and recentActivity returned (default: 5) (number) [min: 1, max: 20]

arguments.name: recursive

tools.description: Run a workflow immediately and create a fresh conversation for it.

arguments.name: workflowId

arguments.name: openInChat

tools.description: Update workspace properties. Pass only fields to change - others remain unchanged.

arguments.name: id
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] Workspace ID or name to update (REQUIRED). This selects the target workspace; using the workspace name is fine and the UUID is not required. (string)

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] New workspace name (optional). This renames the workspace; it is not the target identifier. (string)

arguments.name: description
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] New workspace description (optional) (string)

arguments.name: rootFolder
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] New root folder path (optional, will create if needed) (string)

arguments.name: purpose
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] New workspace purpose (optional, updates context.purpose) (string)

arguments.name: workflows
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] New workflows array (optional, replaces context.workflows) (Array of an object)

arguments.name: keyFiles
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] New key files array (optional, replaces context.keyFiles) (Array of a string)

arguments.name: preferences
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] New preferences text (optional, updates context.preferences) (string)

arguments.name: dedicatedAgentId

tools.description: Generate images using Google Nano Banana models (direct or via OpenRouter). Supports reference images for style/composition guidance.

arguments.name: prompt
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] Text prompt describing the image to generate (string) [min length: 1, max length: 32000]

arguments.name: provider
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] AI provider (default: google). Available: google, openrouter (One of: ["google", "openrouter"])

arguments.name: model
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Image generation model (default: gemini-2.5-flash-image). Available: gemini-2.5-flash-image, gemini-3-pro-image-preview, gemini-3.1-flash-image-preview, gpt-5-image, flux-2-pro, flux-2-flex (One of: ["gemini-2.5-flash-image", "gemini-3-pro-image-preview", "gemini-3.1-flash-image-preview", "gpt-5-image", "flux-2-pro", "flux-2-flex"])

arguments.name: numberOfImages
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Number of images to generate (1-4) (number) [min: 1, max: 4]

arguments.name: imageSize
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Image resolution. 512px only for gemini-3.1-flash-image-preview. 4K available for gemini-3-pro-image-preview and gemini-3.1-flash-image-preview (One of: ["512px", "1K", "2K", "4K"])

arguments.name: referenceImages
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Reference images for style/composition. Max 3 for 2.5-flash, max 14 for 3-pro (Array of a string) [max items: 14]

arguments.name: savePath
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] Vault-relative path for the image. Extension may change based on API response format. (string) [pattern: ^[^/].*\.(png|jpg|jpeg|webp)$]

tools.description: Get a custom prompt for persona adoption - does NOT execute tasks automatically

arguments.name: id
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Unique ID or name of the prompt to retrieve for persona adoption (will try ID first, then name) (string)

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Name of the prompt to retrieve for persona adoption (string)

tools.description: List all custom prompts

arguments.name: enabledOnly
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] If true, only return enabled prompts (boolean)

arguments.name: includeArchived
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] If true, include archived (disabled) prompts in results. Default false filters out archived prompts. (boolean)

tools.description: List available LLM models grouped by provider

Spawn an autonomous subagent to work on a task in the background.

The subagent runs independently, using tools as needed, until it completes.
Results appear as a tool result when the subagent finishes.

Use for:
- Deep research tasks requiring multiple searches
- Analysis tasks that need file reading and processing
- Complex operations you want to run in parallel

The subagent has access to all tools via getTools.

To continue a subagent that hit max iterations, provide continueBranchId.

arguments.name: task
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] Clear description of what the subagent should accomplish (string)

arguments.name: agent
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Optional custom agent/persona name to use (string)

arguments.name: tools
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Hand off specific tools to the subagent. Format: { "agentName": ["tool1", "tool2"] }. Tool schemas are pre-filled in subagent system prompt so it can use useTool directly without calling getTools first. (object)

arguments.name: contextFiles
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] File paths to read - content will be included in the subagent context (Array of a string)

arguments.name: maxIterations
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Maximum iterations before pausing (default: 10) (number)

arguments.name: continueBranchId
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Branch ID to continue from max_iterations state (string)

tools.description: Update an existing custom prompt

arguments.name: id
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] Unique ID of the prompt to update (string) [min length: 1]

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] New name for the prompt (must be unique) (string) [min length: 1, max length: 100]

arguments.name: description
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] New description for the prompt (string) [min length: 1, max length: 500]

arguments.name: prompt
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] New prompt text/persona (string) [min length: 1]

arguments.name: isEnabled
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Whether the prompt is enabled (boolean)

tools.description: Search vault files. Set semantic=true for AI-powered conceptual search using local embeddings (best for concepts/related ideas), or semantic=false for keyword/fuzzy search (best for exact matches). Semantic search is desktop-only and becomes available once the embedding system initializes in the background (first run may take longer while the model downloads).

arguments.name: query
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] Search query to find files and content. (string)

arguments.name: semantic
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] true = AI-powered conceptual search (desktop only, best for concepts/related ideas). false = keyword/fuzzy search (default, best for exact matches). (boolean)

arguments.name: limit
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Maximum number of results to return (default: 10) (number) [min: 1, max: 50]

arguments.name: includeContent
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] For keyword search only: include content snippets (default: true). Ignored for semantic search. (boolean)

arguments.name: snippetLength
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] For keyword search only: length of content snippets (default: 200). Ignored for semantic search. (number) [min: 50, max: 1000]

arguments.name: paths
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Restrict search to specific folder paths. Supports glob patterns. (Array of a string)

tools.description: FOCUSED directory search with REQUIRED paths parameter. Search for files and/or folders within specific directory paths using fuzzy matching and optional workspace context. Requires: query (search terms) and paths (directory paths to search - cannot be empty).

arguments.name: query
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] Search term to find in file/folder names and paths. Fuzzy matching is automatic. (string) [min length: 1]

arguments.name: paths
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] Directory paths to search within. Supports glob patterns. Use ["/"] for entire vault. (Array of a string) [min items: 1]

arguments.name: searchType
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] What to search for: "files", "folders", or "both" (One of: ["files", "folders", "both"])

arguments.name: fileTypes
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Filter by file extensions without dots (Array of a string)

arguments.name: depth
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Maximum directory depth to search (1-10) (number) [min: 1, max: 10]

arguments.name: pattern
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Regular expression pattern to filter paths (string)

arguments.name: dateRange
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Filter results by modification date range (object)

arguments.name: limit
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Maximum number of results (1-100) (number) [min: 1, max: 100]

arguments.name: includeContent
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Include content snippets from files (boolean)

tools.description:
Search workspace memory for past conversations, tool execution history, and workspace state snapshots.

TWO MODES:
- Discovery (default): Search all memory across a workspace. Best for finding past discussions, tool usage, or workspace context.
- Scoped (provide sessionId or sessionName): Search within a specific session and get surrounding message context around each match. Best for recovering what happened in a particular session.

TIPS:
- Use natural language queries for conversations (e.g., "how did we implement auth?").
- Use specific terms for tool history (e.g., agent or tool names).
- Narrow results with memoryTypes if you know what you need.
- Use sessionName + windowSize to get full context around a named session match.

REQUIRES: query. Optional: workspaceId accepts the workspace name from load-workspace; omit it to search the global workspace.

arguments.name: query
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] What to search for. Use natural language for conversations ('how did we handle auth?') or specific terms for tool history ('contentManager read'). Examples: 'authentication implementation', 'database migration error', 'what tools were used for file editing' (string) [min length: 1]

arguments.name: memoryTypes
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Which memory to search. 'conversations' = past chat Q&A pairs, 'traces' = tool execution history, 'sessions' = named work sessions, 'states' = workspace snapshots. Defaults to all available types. Narrow to specific types if you know what you need. (Array of a string)

arguments.name: sessionName
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Human-readable session name returned by load-workspace. Use this to scope search to a named session without changing the top-level chat sessionId. (string)

arguments.name: windowSize
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Number of conversation turns before/after each match to include. Default 3. Only used in scoped mode (when sessionId is provided). (number) [min: 1, max: 20]

arguments.name: dateRange
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Filter results by date range (object)

arguments.name: limit
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Maximum number of results to return (number) [min: 1, max: 100]

arguments.name: toolCallFilters
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Additional filters for tool call traces (object)

arguments.name: searchMethod
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] How to match results. 'mixed' (default, recommended) combines approaches for best coverage. 'semantic' prioritizes meaning-based matching. 'exact' requires literal keyword matches. (One of: ["semantic", "exact", "mixed"])

tools.description: Safely archive a file or folder (moves to .archive/ with timestamp)

arguments.name: path
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] Path to file or folder to archive (string)

tools.description: Duplicate a file

arguments.name: path
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] Source file path (string)

arguments.name: newPath
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] Destination path for the copy (string)

arguments.name: overwrite
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Overwrite if destination exists (default: false) (boolean)

tools.description: Create a new folder in the vault

arguments.name: path
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] Path of the folder to create (REQUIRED) (string)

tools.description: List contents of a directory

arguments.name: path
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Directory path (optional). Use empty string (""), "/" or "." for vault root. Defaults to vault root. (string)

arguments.name: filter
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Optional filter pattern. Supports glob patterns (* for any chars, ? for single char) or regular expressions. Examples: "*deep*", "test*", ".*regex.*" (string)

tools.description: Move or rename a file or folder

arguments.name: path
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] Source path (file or folder) (string)

arguments.name: newPath
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] Destination path (string)

arguments.name: overwrite
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Overwrite if destination exists (default: false) (boolean)

tools.description: Open a file in the editor

arguments.name: path
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] Path to the file to open (string)

arguments.name: mode
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Where to open the file (tab, split, window, or current) (One of: ["tab", "split", "window", "current"])

arguments.name: focus
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Whether to focus the opened file (boolean)

tools.description: Archive a project by setting its status to 'archived' (soft-delete). The project and its tasks remain queryable but are excluded from active listings. Requires a projectId (from createProject or listProjects).

arguments.name: projectId
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] Project ID to archive (REQUIRED — from createProject or listProjects) (string)

tools.description: Create a task within a project. Requires a projectId (from createProject or listProjects). Supports optional priority (critical/high/medium/low), assignee, dueDate, tags, dependsOn[] for DAG edges (cycles rejected), parentTaskId for subtask nesting, and linkedNotes[] for vault note links. Returns the new taskId.

arguments.name: projectId
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] Project ID to create the task in (REQUIRED — from createProject or listProjects) (string)

arguments.name: title
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] Task title (REQUIRED) (string)

arguments.name: description
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Task description (optional) (string)

arguments.name: parentTaskId
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Parent task ID to nest this task under as a subtask (optional — from createTask or listTasks) (string)

arguments.name: priority
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Task priority (default: medium) (One of: ["critical", "high", "medium", "low"])

arguments.name: dueDate
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Due date as Unix timestamp in milliseconds (e.g., Date.now() + 86400000 for tomorrow) (number)

arguments.name: assignee
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Assignee name or identifier (optional) (string)

arguments.name: tags
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Tags for categorization (optional) (Array of a string)

arguments.name: dependsOn
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Task IDs this task depends on — creates DAG edges. Task cannot start until all dependencies are done. Cycles are rejected with an error. (Array of a string)

arguments.name: linkedNotes
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Vault note paths to link to this task (link type defaults to reference) (Array of a string)

arguments.name: metadata
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Custom metadata key-value pairs (optional) (object)

tools.description: Create a new project within a workspace. Projects organize tasks and must have a unique name per workspace. Requires a workspaceId (from loadWorkspace or createWorkspace). Returns the new projectId.

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] Project name (REQUIRED — must be unique within the workspace) (string)

arguments.name: description
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Project description (optional) (string)

arguments.name: metadata
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Custom metadata key-value pairs (optional) (object)

tools.description: Create or remove a bidirectional link between a vault note and a task. Link types: reference (related note), output (task produces this note), input (task consumes this note). Use action=unlink to remove an existing link.

arguments.name: taskId
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] Task ID to link the note to (REQUIRED — from createTask or listTasks) (string)

arguments.name: notePath
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] Vault note path, e.g. "folder/note.md" (REQUIRED) (string)

arguments.name: linkType
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Type of link (default: reference). reference=related note, output=task produces this note, input=task consumes this note (One of: ["reference", "output", "input"])

arguments.name: action
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Action to perform (default: link) (One of: ["link", "unlink"])

tools.description: List tasks in a project with optional filters for status (todo/in_progress/done/cancelled), priority, assignee, and parentTaskId. Returns paginated task objects with full metadata including dependencies and timestamps.

arguments.name: projectId
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] Project ID (REQUIRED — from createProject or listProjects) (string)

arguments.name: status
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Filter by task status (One of: ["todo", "in_progress", "done", "cancelled"])

arguments.name: priority
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Filter by priority (One of: ["critical", "high", "medium", "low"])

arguments.name: assignee
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Filter by assignee (string)

arguments.name: parentTaskId
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Filter by parent task (subtasks of this task) (string)

arguments.name: includeSubtasks
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Include subtasks in results (default: true) (boolean)

arguments.name: sortBy
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Sort field (default: updated) (One of: ["created", "updated", "priority", "title", "dueDate"])

arguments.name: sortOrder
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Sort direction (default: desc) (One of: ["asc", "desc"])

arguments.name: page
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Page number (0-indexed, default: 0) (number) [min: 0]

arguments.name: pageSize
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Items per page (default: 20) (number) [min: 1, max: 100]

tools.description: List projects in a workspace with optional status filter (active/completed/archived). Returns paginated project objects with id, name, description, status, and timestamps. Use to discover projectIds for task operations.

arguments.name: status
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Filter by project status (One of: ["active", "completed", "archived"])

arguments.name: page
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Page number (0-indexed, default: 0) (number) [min: 0]

arguments.name: pageSize
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Items per page (default: 20) (number) [min: 1, max: 100]

tools.description: Move a task to a different project within the same workspace, or change its parent task (set parentTaskId to nest as subtask, null to make top-level). Requires a taskId (from createTask or listTasks).

arguments.name: taskId
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] Task ID to move (REQUIRED — from createTask or listTasks) (string)

arguments.name: projectId
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Target project ID to move the task to (from createProject or listProjects — must be in the same workspace) (string)

arguments.name: parentTaskId
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] New parent task ID (null to make top-level, string to nest under another task) (string | null)

tools.description: Open the native Task Board workspace view in Obsidian. Optional filters let you preselect a workspace, project, or search query before the board is shown.

arguments.name: projectId
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Optional project ID to preselect in the board filter. (string)

arguments.name: search
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Optional search query to prefill in the board search input. (string)

arguments.name: mode
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Where to open the task board view. (One of: ["tab", "split", "current", "sidebar"])

tools.description: DAG-aware queries on a project's tasks. Three query types: nextActions returns tasks ready to start (status=todo and all dependencies done), blockedTasks returns tasks waiting on incomplete dependencies with their blocker details, dependencyTree returns the full upstream/downstream dependency graph for a specific task. Requires projectId; dependencyTree also requires taskId.

arguments.name: projectId
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] Project ID to query (REQUIRED — from createProject or listProjects) (string)

arguments.name: query
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] Query type (REQUIRED). nextActions: tasks with status=todo whose dependencies are all done — these are ready to start. blockedTasks: tasks waiting on incomplete dependencies, returned with their blocker details. dependencyTree: full upstream/downstream dependency graph for a specific task (requires taskId). (One of: ["nextActions", "blockedTasks", "dependencyTree"])

arguments.name: taskId
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Task ID (REQUIRED for dependencyTree query — from createTask or listTasks) (string)

tools.description: Update task fields (title, description, status, priority, dueDate, assignee, tags), manage DAG dependencies (addDependencies/removeDependencies), and manage note links (addNoteLinks/removeNoteLinks). Dependency additions are validated for cycles. Requires a taskId (from createTask or listTasks).

arguments.name: taskId
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] Task ID to update (REQUIRED — from createTask or listTasks) (string)

arguments.name: title
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] New task title (string)

arguments.name: description
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] New task description (string)

arguments.name: status
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] New task status (setting to done auto-records completedAt timestamp) (One of: ["todo", "in_progress", "done", "cancelled"])

arguments.name: priority
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] New task priority (One of: ["critical", "high", "medium", "low"])

arguments.name: dueDate
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] New due date as Unix timestamp in milliseconds (e.g., Date.now() + 86400000 for tomorrow) (number)

arguments.name: assignee
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] New assignee name or identifier (string)

arguments.name: tags
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Replace entire tags array with these values (Array of a string)

arguments.name: addDependencies
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Task IDs to add as DAG dependencies — this task cannot start until these are done. Cycles are rejected with an error. (Array of a string)

arguments.name: removeDependencies
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Task IDs to remove from this task's dependencies (Array of a string)

arguments.name: addNoteLinks
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Vault notes to link to this task (Array of an object)

arguments.name: removeNoteLinks
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Vault note paths to unlink from this task (Array of a string)

arguments.name: metadata
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Custom metadata to merge (keys are merged, not replaced) (object)

tools.description: Update a project's name, description, status (active/completed/archived), or custom metadata. Requires a projectId (from createProject or listProjects).

arguments.name: projectId
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] Project ID to update (REQUIRED — from createProject or listProjects) (string)

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] New project name (string)

arguments.name: description
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] New project description (string)

arguments.name: status
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] New project status (One of: ["active", "completed", "archived"])

arguments.name: metadata
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Custom metadata to merge (object)

tools.description: Save the active Web Viewer page to the vault as Markdown using Obsidian Web Viewer. Desktop only.

arguments.name: url
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Optional URL to open in Web Viewer before capturing. If omitted, captures the active Web Viewer tab. (string)

arguments.name: mode
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Where to open the Web Viewer tab when url is provided. (One of: ["tab", "split", "window", "current"])

arguments.name: outputPath
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] Destination note path in the vault. Required so the caller explicitly chooses where the Markdown capture is saved. (string)

arguments.name: timeoutMs
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Maximum time to wait for page load and note creation. (number)

arguments.name: settleMs
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Extra delay after page load before invoking Save to vault. (number)

tools.description: Print the current Web Viewer page to PDF and save it to the vault. Desktop only.

arguments.name: url
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Optional URL to open in Web Viewer before capturing. (string)

arguments.name: mode
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Where to open the Web Viewer tab when url is provided. (One of: ["tab", "split", "window", "current"])

arguments.name: outputPath
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] Destination PDF path in the vault. Required so the caller explicitly chooses where the document is saved. (string)

arguments.name: timeoutMs
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Maximum time to wait for page load. (number)

arguments.name: settleMs
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Extra delay after page load before printing. (number)

tools.description: Capture the current Web Viewer page as a PNG image and save it to the vault. Desktop only.

arguments.name: url
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Optional URL to open in Web Viewer before capturing. (string)

arguments.name: mode
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Where to open the Web Viewer tab when url is provided. (One of: ["tab", "split", "window", "current"])

arguments.name: outputPath
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] Destination PNG path in the vault. Required so the caller explicitly chooses where the image is saved. (string)

arguments.name: timeoutMs
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Maximum time to wait for page load. (number)

arguments.name: settleMs
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Extra delay after page load before capturing. (number)

tools.description: Extract links from the current Web Viewer page. Desktop only.

arguments.name: url
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Optional URL to open in Web Viewer before extracting links. (string)

arguments.name: mode
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Where to open the Web Viewer tab when url is provided. (One of: ["tab", "split", "window", "current"])

arguments.name: maxLinks
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Maximum number of links to return. (number)

arguments.name: timeoutMs
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Maximum time to wait for page load. (number)

arguments.name: settleMs
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Extra delay after page load before extracting links. (number)

tools.description: Open a webpage in Obsidian Web Viewer. Desktop only.

arguments.name: url
arguments.description: [REQUIRED] The webpage URL to open in Obsidian Web Viewer. (string)

arguments.name: mode
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Where to open the Web Viewer tab. (One of: ["tab", "split", "window", "current"])

arguments.name: focus
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Whether to focus the Web Viewer tab after opening it. (boolean)

arguments.name: timeoutMs
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Maximum time to wait for the page to load. (number)

arguments.name: settleMs
arguments.description: [OPTIONAL] Extra delay after load before returning, to allow client-side rendering to settle. (number)
